subject: Project Management
title: Schedule Risk
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Schedule Risk.

hook.text: Schedule risk is the chance your project won't finish on time. It's the most common risk of all.

buildup.text: Late requirements, unexpected dependencies, and key person absences all push schedules out.

discovery.text: The critical path is where schedule risk concentrates — any delay there delays the entire project.

twist.text: Padding every task creates false confidence. Smart teams add buffers to the path, not to individual tasks.

climax.text: Schedule risk is rarely about one big delay — it's dozens of small slippages that compound silently.

punchline.text: Projects don't slip a month. They slip a day at a time.

quiz.question: How do schedule delays typically occur?
options: Through dozens of small slippages that compound over time
Only when the budget runs out
